switch.description: When set to -1, only the gradient to the left/bottom is used to compute the conductive neutral flux limits. When set to 0, only the gradient to the left/bottom is used to compute the conductive neutral flux limits, except when these do not exist (the other face value is used).

switch.name: b2trno_flux_limit_to_dpa
switch.description: If '1', flux limit to neutrals contribution to dpa0 - the diffusion coefficient is applied in b2tlc0.F. It is recommended '1'. b2tlc0.F has the flux limit parameters alpha and gamma which are given by 'b2tlc0_alpha' and 'b2tlc0_gamma'. 'b2tfnb_alpha' and 'b2tlc0_alpha' cannot be different from zero simultaneously. 'b2tfnb_alpha' gives another form of flux limit which is applied to the whole particle flux.

switchgroup.name: b2mndr_*fb*
switchgroup.description: Experimental: allows for density feedback of the number of electrons accompanying species 'isfb' at location (ixfb,iyfb). Note that ixfb and iyfb are real numbers!

switch.name: b2mndt_use_b2srst
switch.description: Switches off the stabilization of the source coefficients.

switch.name: b2mndt_rxf
switch.description: Main under-relaxation parameter.

switchgroup.name: b2news_xfm.
switchgroup.description: Under-relaxation parameters for the parallel momentum, continuity, potential and energy equations respectively.

switchgroup.name: b2npco_pcm.
switchgroup.description: pcm0 specifies an additional under-relaxation factor that is applied to the velocity correction. It is required that 0.le.pcm0.and.0.le.pcm1.

switch.name: b2npco_rxg
switch.description: rxg specifies a special under-relaxation parameter. A term of the form "abs(residual)/rxg" is added to the diagonal of the matrix of the correction equation, with the effect of limiting the computed correction. rxg is dimensionless and of order unity; smaller values of rxg imply stronger damping.

switchgroup.name: b2npht_pcm*
switchgroup.description: pcm0 specifies an additional under-relaxation factor that is applied to the temperature correction. It is required that 0.le.pcm0.and.0.le.pcm1.

switch.name: b2npht_rxg
switch.description: rxg specifies a special under-relaxation parameter. A term of the form "abs(residual)/rxg" is added to the diagonal of the matrix of the correction equation, with the effect of limiting the computed correction. rxg is dimensionless and of order unity; smaller values of rxg imply stronger damping.

switchgroup.name: b2nph*
switchgroup.description: When set to '1', SPb's form of the program b2sihs_ is called. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2npp7_style
switch.description: When set to '1', SPb's form of the program b2usp7_ is called. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2npmo_rxg
switch.description: Normalisation factor for the parallel momentum equation.

switch.name: b2news_poteq
switch.description: If poteq.eq.0, the potential equation is jumped over and not solved. If poteq.eq.2, the potential is set to 3.1*Te/qe as per SOLPS4.0. If poteq.eq.1, the potential equation is solved according to the no_solve switch settings. If poteq.ne.1, then 'b2tfhe_no_current'must be set to '1'.

switch.name: b2nxdv_style
switch.description: When set to '1', the total friction force cancel is not calculated at the guard boundary cells. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2nxfc_style
switch.description: style specifies the precise form of interpolation used in the computation of flcb and cvcb. When set to '1', SPb's form of the transport terms in the momentum correction equation is used. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2nxfx_style
switch.description: When set to '1', SPb's form of an expression that occurs in the electron-atom thermal force is used. It is recommended '1'.

switchgroup.name: b2sifr_styl*
switchgroup.description: Specify the type of linearisation used in the thermal force term.

switch.name: b2sigp_style
switch.description: When set to '1', SPb's form of the pressure gradient term on the right hand of the momentum balance equation is used. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2xzdd_zero_dead_and_core
switch.description: If 1 then zero passed sources in dead regions, If 2 zero passed sources in dead regions and core boundary cells If 0 then SKIP

switchgroup.name: b2sihs_rf.
switchgroup.description: rf0-4 are numerical parameters, see the code. All must be non-negative and of order unity; larger values are meant to provide more stabilisation. Some or all may be set to zero and removed eventually. I have had some experience to indicate that rf0, rf2, rf4 may have a useful function; however, later changes in the code may have made all these parameters superfluous.

switch.name: b2sihs_style
switch.description: style determines the form of the strange electron-atom energy transfer term.

switchgroup.name: b2srdt_phm.
switchgroup.description: Multipliers to the density, parallel momentum, heat, potential, and electron prticle time-derivative source terms, respectively. 'phm4' is normally zero since the potential equation contains no source terms.

switchgroup.name: b2srst_rf*
switchgroup.description: (rf0/1/2/3 for numerical stabilisation)

switch.name: b2stcx_rg0
switch.description: (rg0 for numerical stabilisation; needs experiments.)

switch.name: b2stcx_styl0
switch.description: Specifies the type of linearisation used in the charge exchange momentum source term.

switch.name: eirene_mc_linearisation
switch.description: Specifies the type of linearisation used in the sources derived from the Monte-Carlo neutrals. If linearisation.eq.0, all sources are fed to the constant term, if linearisation.eq.1, positive terms go in the constant term, and negative terms in the proportional term. 'eirene_mc_linearization' is an alias for this switch.

switch.name: b2stbm_linearisation
switch.description: Same as above, but applied to the sources coming for an externally coupled code providing plasma sources, for instance a kinetic treatment of trace impurity ions. 'b2stbm_linearization' is an alias for this switch.

switch.name: b2stbm_impgyro_mod
switch.description: Specifies the frequency (in units of full b2 timesteps) at which an externally coupled code providing additional source (for instance a kinetic treatment of trace impurity ions such as IMPGYRO) should be called.

switchgroup.name: b2stel_r*
switchgroup.description: Damping coefficient applied to the atomic physics sources.

switch.name: b2stel_styl0
switch.description: Specifies the type of linearisation used in the charge exchange momentum source term.

switch.name: b2tfcc_xfac
switch.description: Multiplier to the pressure force term.

switch.name: b2tfhe_mdf
switch.description: If '1', Spb's new form of calculating electron heat flux is used. It is recommended '1' for runs with drifts.

switch.name: b2tfhe_no_current
switch.description: If no_current.eq.1, all currents are set to zero. The setting no_current.eq.1 must be used if the potential equation is not explicitly solved for, i.e. for b2news_poteq.ne.1.

switch.name: b2tfhi_mdf
switch.description: If '1', Spb's new form of calculating ion heat flux is used. It is recommended '1' for runs with drifts.

switch.name: b2tfnb_drift_style
switch.description: When set to '0', drift velocities are calculated in cell centers. When set to '1', drift velocities are calculated in cell faces. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2tfnb_fnb_nodrift_style
switch.description: When set to '1', SPb's form of calculating no drift part of the particle fluxes is used. It is recommended '1'.

switch.name: b2tfnb_mdf
switch.description: If '1', Spb's new form of calculating particle flux is used. It is recommended '1' for runs with drifts.

switch.name: b2tfnb_xfrhie
switch.description: Multiplier to the Rhie and Chow upwind correction.

switch.name: b2upht_stylec
switch.description: (stylec is a numerical switch, needs experiments)

switch.name: b2usmo_cfc0
switch.description: Linearisation constant.

switch.name: b2srsm_enable
switch.description: If enable.ne.0, then the sources for particle, parallel momentum, electron and ion heat are rescaled, for non-boundary cells, if the local timestep exceeds the physics timescale implied by those sources.

switch.name: b2ardr_fix_cx
switch.description: It is used to "correct" the CX data 0 => do not fix 1 => only fix H if CX data is < 1e-40 [default] 2 => fix if CX data is < 1e-40 3 => fix H 4 => fix all At the moment the only species with CX data in ADAS is C. Be careful with options that use the fit formula other than for H. See the comments in ratstr.F for the origin of the fit formula used to "fix" the CX data.

switch.name: b2ardr_no_weisheit
switch.description: When set to 1, disables the correction of the hydrogen atomic rate data with the Weisheit data using ratwei. *** Use with caution! ***

switch.name: b2ardr_no_smoothing
switch.description: When set to 1, disables the tail smoothing for of rate coefficients to represent the effect of a power-law tail on the distribution function. It has been added for compatibility with SOLPS4.3

switch.name: b2ardr_fix_recomb
switch.description: When changed from 0, and if the ADAS option is in use, then uses the PRB file to add the contribution arising from is-->is-1 processes. This term includes the bremsstrahlung. The default option ('0') only contains the Bremsstrahlung for the is-->is-1 process. This option should be used with the 'b2stel_fix_recomb_energy' option b2mn.dat set to '1'. See 'Physics' section. *** Use with caution! ***

switchgroup.name: b2ardr_rtn.
switchgroup.description: The number of intervals in the discretisation of the density (rtnn) and temperature (rtnt) ranges for the atomic physics tables.

switchgroup.name: b2mndr_dpc_mod_rates_*_hot*
switchgroup.description: Modify the atomic rates by including a hot electron population of temperature te_hot (in eV) and a population faction ne_hot_frac. Still experimental.

switch.name: NSTRAI
switch.description: Number of neutral sources, or 'strata'. Must not be larger than DEF_NSTRA from $(SOLPSTOP)/include(.local)/DIMENSIONS.F file. Need not include the time-dependent stratum. If a time-dependent stratum is used, is incremented internally as needed. The incremented value is referred to as NSTRAT below.

switch.name: RCPOS
switch.description: Position in the B2 grid of the of strata. Similar use as BCPOS from /BOUNDARY/ namelist.

switch.name: RCSTART
switch.description: Start coordinate on the B2 grid the strata. Similar use as BCSTART from /BOUNDARY/ namelist.

switch.name: RCEND
switch.description: End coordinate on the B2 grid of the strata. Similar use as BCEND from /BOUNDARY/ namelist.

switch.name: RC_LIST_SIZE
switch.description: Contains the size of the recycling boundary lists. Similar use as BC_LIST_SIZE from /BOUNDARY/ namelist.

switch.name: RC_LIST_X
switch.description: Contains the X-coordinate of the cells where recycling boundaries are applied. Similar use as BC_LIST_X from /BOUNDARY/ namelist.

switch.name: TARGSP
switch.description: Identifies the base material(s) of this stratum wall. The number corresponds to the B2 species index produced by sputtering. Check b2cdci for details. If the bulk species is not sputtered, should contain -1.

switch.name: CHEMSP
switch.description: Indicates whether chemical sputtering is allowed from this wall stratum.

switch.name: RECYC
switch.description: Stores the particle recycling coefficients of species (is) on stratum (istra). Defaults to 1.0 for non-carbon species and 0.0 for carbon species. Particles recycle into the neutral species associated to their homonuclear sequence. Applies to B2 neutral fluid species. Also multiplies Eirene recycling fluxes if 'eirene_use_recyceir' is set to 0 (see b2cdci for details).

switch.name: MRECYC
switch.description: Stores the parallel momentum recycling coefficients of species (is) on stratum (istra). The parallel momentum is carried back by the recycled neutrals. Applies only to B2 neutral fluid species.

switch.name: ERECYC
switch.description: Stores the energy recycling coefficients of species (is) on stratum (istra). Defaults to 0.3 for non-carbon species and 0.0 for carbon species. The energy is carried back by the recycled neutrals. Applies only to B2 neutral fluid species.

switch.name: RCION
switch.description: Stores the particle-into-ion recycling coefficients of species (is) on stratum (istra). Particles recycle into the next ionised species associated to their homonuclear sequence. Applies only to B2 neutral fluid species.

switch.name: RECYCEIR
switch.description: Multiplier to the Eirene recycling fluxes from stratum (istra). Only used if 'eirene_use_recyceir' is set to 1 (default).

switch.name: USERFLUXPARM
switch.description: The element (istra,1) contains the strength of constant gas puff strata (type 'C') for Eirene in particles/second.

switch.name: CRCSTRA
switch.description: Contains the type of stratum for Eirene. . Possible options include: 'N','S','W','E' - topological mesh boundaries (same use as BCCHAR in /BOUNDARY/ namelist) 'V' - volume recombination source (related RCPOS, RCSTART, RCEND and RC_LIST variables are ignored). 'C' - constant or feedback gas puff source (related RCPOS, RCSTART, RCEND and RC_LIST variables are ignored). Gas puffs for B2 fluid neutrals are handled via b2.boundary.parameters. Unless specified otherwise by use of 'eirene_nesepm_istra', it is the first 'C' stratum that is used for feedback puff schemes. See b2cdci for details. 'T' - time-dependent source for Eirene (related RCPOS, RCSTART, RCEND and RC_LIST variables are ignored). Long-lived Eirene neutrals are stored in this stratum. See EIRENE_STEP_DT below.

switch.name: RF_NEUT
switch.description: Relaxation parameters multiplying the Eirene particle, parallel momentum, electron energy and ion energy sources respectively before use in B2.

switch.name: PHYS_SPUT
switch.description: Stores the physical sputtering multiplier for B2 species (is) sputtering on the wall of stratum (istra). Must be non-zero for physical sputtering to occur. Only applies if using B2 fluid neutral model.

switch.name: CHEM_SPUT
switch.description: Stores the chemical sputtering multiplier for B2 species (is) sputtering on the wall of stratum (istra). Must be non-zero for chemical sputtering to occur. Only applies if using B2 fluid neutral model.

switch.name: EIRENE_STEP_CPU
switch.description: Length of CPU time devoted to Eirene calls after the first one (in s). Defaults to the value given in input.dat.

switch.name: EIRENE_STEP_DT
switch.description: Physical time (in s) the Eirene particles are to be followed before being passed to the time-dependent stratum.

switch.name: EIRENE_MOD
switch.description: Frequency of Eirene calls. Eirene is called every EIRENE_MOD full B2 iterations.

switch.name: VOLRECSTART
switch.description: Initial volume recombination rate from stratum (istra) to be used for the volume recombination scaling regulation algorithm. Rendered obsolete by 'eirene_dpc_fix'.

switch.name: VOLRECINC
switch.description: Maximum rate of increase of the volume recombination source strength. The volume recombination regulation scaling scheme is activated if VOLRECINC is neither 0 nor 1. Rendered obsolete by 'eirene_dpc_fix'.

switch.name: VOLRECWT
switch.description: Weight of new volume recombination strength relative to that of previous iteration in the volume recombination regulation scaling scheme.

switch.name: SPECIES_START
switch.description: Specifies the index of the first B2 species involved in stratum (istra).

switch.name: SPECIES_END
switch.description: Specifies the index of the last B2 species involved in stratum (istra).

switch.name: NEUTRALS_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /NEUTRALS/ namelist.

switch.name: NEUTRALS_TIME_MOD
switch.description: Time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle after which a new NEUTRALS namelist is read.

switch.name: L_NEUTRAD
switch.description: If l_neutrad >= 0, then the radiated power due to the neutrals atoms is taken directly from the Eirene calculation, instead of being recomputed by B2.

switch.name: L_NEUTFLUX
switch.description: If l_neutflux >=0, then correct treatment of the incident fluxes in B2 and b2plot; if <0, then old (approximate) treatment

switch.name: LSTRASCL
switch.description: Indicates with which Eirene atomic species to scale the Eirene stratum (istra) (0 means no scaling, default). Atomic species 0 stands for electrons.

switch.name: B2ESPCR
switch.default: ordering of the B2 isonuclear sequences
switch.description: Contains the index of the Eirene atomic species corresponding to the B2 species (is).

switch.name: EB2SPCR
switch.default: first B2 species of each isonuclear sequence
switch.description: Contains the index of the B2 neutral fluid species corresponding to the Eirene atomic species (iatm).

switch.name: LMOLSCL
switch.description: Contains the index of the Eirene atomic species with which the Eirene molecular species (IMOL) should be scaled.

switch.name: MLCMP
switch.description: Contains the number of atoms from Eirene atomic species IATM within each molecule of Eirene molecular species IMOL.

switch.name: LIONSCL
switch.description: Contains the index of the Eirene atomic species with which the Eirene test ion species (IION) should be scaled.

switch.name: LCNS
switch.description: Contains the indices of Eirene non-standard surfaces (block 3A in input.dat) corresponding to core boundaries.

switch.name: LTNS
switch.description: Contains the indices of Eirene non-standard surfaces (block 3A in input.dat) corresponding to target boundaries.

switch.name: LSNS
switch.description: Contains the indices of Eirene surfaces related to the recycling sources.

switch.name: KSNS
switch.description: Contains the number of parts for each Eirene recycling stratum.

switch.name: GPFC
switch.description: Specifies the fraction of Eirene atomic species (iatm) in one particle puffed from stratum (istra).

switch.name: DBG_EIR_MC
switch.description: Debug output control for eirene_mc routine. See code for usage.

switch.name: DEBUG_FLAGS
switch.description: Non-zero elements yield additional print-out data for debugging B2-Eirene coupling. See code for specific uses. Many slots still available for user-specific needs.

switch.name: NEUT_SCL_LIM
switch.description: Maximum number by which Eirene neutral sources may be scaled in either direction within the ank-mods scheme. See explaining text in $SOLPSTOP/doc/Source_Scaling_in_B2.pdf for details.

switch.name: TRACK_INDEX
switch.description: Specifies the mixed material species index related to B2 species (is).

switch.name: TRACK_CHEM_SPUT
switch.description: Indicates whether mixed material species (itrack) participates in chemical sputtering. Defaults to .true. for first tracked species if sput_dst is carbon, and to .false. for all other cases.

switch.name: CHEMICAL_EROSION_REDEP_FAC
switch.description: Multiplier to the chemical sputtering coefficient of carbon for computing the rate used for redeposited carbon.

switch.name: CHEMICAL_EROSION_BE_FAC
switch.description: Indicates whether the presence of beryllium is to impact on the chemical sputtering yield of carbon.

switch.name: CHEMICAL_EROSION_BE_FAC_A
switch.description: The chemical sputtering yield of carbon is multiplied by (1.0-C/2*(tanh((frac-A)/B)-tanh((-A)/B))) where frac is the fractional content of Be in the surface layer material.

switch.name: CHEMICAL_EROSION_BE_FAC_B
switch.description: See CHEMICAL_EROSION_BE_FAC_A.

switch.name: CHEMICAL_EROSION_BE_FAC_C
switch.description: See CHEMICAL_EROSION_BE_FAC_A.

switch.name: N_SPCSRF
switch.description: Number of special surfaces groups for diagnostics.

switch.name: L_SPCSRF
switch.description: List of surface segments (non-default standard surfaces [NDSS] or additional surfaces in Eirene notation) included in the groups. Negative numbers correspond to NDSS.

switch.name: SPS_ID
switch.description: Labels of groups of special surfaces.

switch.name: I_SPCSRF
switch.description: Index of the first Eirene surface belonging to a special surface group in the L_SPCSRF list.

switch.name: J_SPCSRF
switch.description: Index of the last Eirene surface belonging to a special surface group in the L_SPCSRF list.

switch.name: SPS_ABSR
switch.description: Particle absorption on the surface. If positive, will supercede the setting from the Eirene input file: RECYCT = 1-SPS_ABSR. Ignored if negative.

switch.name: SPS_TRNO
switch.description: Surface transparency in positive direction. If positive, will supercede the setting from the Eirene input file: TRANSP(1,) = SPS_TRNO. Ignored if negative.

switch.name: SPS_TRNI
switch.description: Surface transparency in negative direction. If positive, will supercede the setting from the Eirene input file: TRANSP(2,) = SPS_TRNI. If negative, the setting from SPS_TRNO is used.

switch.name: SPS_MTRI
switch.description: Surface material in Eirene notation (e.g., 1206 for C). If positive, will supercede the setting from the Eirene input file: ZNML = SPS_MTRI. Ignored if negative.

switch.name: SPS_MTRL
switch.description: Surface material in human notation (e.g., 'C').

switch.name: SPS_TMPR
switch.description: Surface temperature (in eV). If set larger to 1.e10, will be ignored. Otherwise, will supercede the setting from the Eirene input file: EWALL = SPS_TMPR.

switch.name: SPS_SPPH
switch.description: Fudge factor for physical sputtering. If positive, will supercede the setting from the Eirene input file: RECYCS = SPS_SPPH. Ignored if negative.

switch.name: SPS_SPCH
switch.description: Fudge factor for chemical sputtering. If positive, will supercede the setting from the Eirene input file: RECYCC = SPS_SPCH. Ignored if negative.

switch.name: SPS_SGRP
switch.description: Sputtered particle species flag for chemical sputtering. If positive, will supercede the setting from the Eirene input file: ISRC = SPS_SGRP. Ignored if negative.

switch.name: WRITE_NML_NEUT
switch.description: If .true. (default), writes the content of the namelist to stdout after it has been read.

switch.name: TIME_DEP_PUFF
switch.description: Indicates whether a stratum is a time-dependent gas puff. Should only be .true. for strata defined as gas puffs.

switch.name: NGPDATA
switch.description: Number of points over which the time profile of the strength of gas puff (istra) is given. Defaults to 0. Current maximum value is 100.

switch.name: GPDATA
switch.description: For (i,ik,istra) in (1:NGPDATA(istra),1:2,1:NSTRAT), GPDATA(i,1,istra) contains the time point (i) for stratum (istra) (in s). GPDATA(i,2,istra) contains the gas puff strength of stratum (istra) attime point (i) (in particles/s). The gas puff strength before the first time point is given by USERFLUXPARM(1,istra). The gas puff strength after the last time point is given by the GPDATA value of the last time point. Otherwise, the gas puff strength is linearly interpolated from the given data.

switch.name: CHEMICAL_SPUTTER_YIELD
switch.description: Passed to Eirene. Chemical sputter yield of wall surface (ilim).

switch.name: FCHAR_CHEMICAL
switch.description: Nuclear charge of atomic species causing the sputtering.

switch.name: IGASS_CHEMICAL
switch.description: Passed to Eirene. Eirene atomic species index of the sputtered particle. If igass_chemical > natmi, the data from chemical_sputter_yield is not used and the yield from the Eirene surface blocks is used instead.

switch.name: ITSPUT_CHEMICAL
switch.description: Passed to Eirene. Eirene type index of the sputtered particle. Atoms = 1, Ions = 4. Defaults to 0, meaning 1 eV atom chemical sputtering.

switch.name: ISSPUT_CHEMICAL
switch.description: Mass*100 + nuclear charge of the sputtered particle. Defaults to 0, internally changed to 1206 = carbon.

switch.name: NDEPTH_NML
switch.description: Dimension NDEPTH used for the arrays within this namelist. Represents the number of depth layer discretising the wall elements for the wall model. If using the 0-D model or the time-independent 1-D model, will contain 1 (default). Should not exceed the value of the parameter NDEPTH declared in b2mod_wall.F.

switch.name: IMAPX
switch.description: Indicates the (ix) position in the grid of wall element (iwall). Default ordering of the wall elements proceeds clockwise in physical space from the lower left corner.

switch.name: IMAPY
switch.description: XYMAP(ix,iy) contains the wall index (iwall) of the wall element located a grid locaion (ix,iy). If there is no wall element at this position, contains 0. Default ordering of the wall elements proceeds clockwise in physical space from the lower left corner.

switch.name: SURFACE_MATERIAL_NAME
switch.description: Contains the filename from which to extract the surface material properties for wall element (iwall). The files are to be found in $(SOLPSTOP)/data(.local)/Surface_properties/.

switch.name: COATING_MATERIAL_NAME
switch.description: Contains the filename from which to extract the bulk material properties of the eventual coating for wall element (iwall).

switch.name: BULK_MATERIAL_NAME
switch.description: Contains the filename from which to extract the bulk material properties for wall element (iwall). $(SOLPSTOP)/data(.local)/Bulk_properties/.

switch.name: LAYER_ALLOYS
switch.description: Contains the filename from which to extract the material properties for alloy (nalloy) which may bw present in mixed materials deposited layers. Not yet operational. The files are to be found in $(SOLPSTOP)/data(.local)/Bulk_properties/ and $(SOLPSTOP)/data(.local)/Surface_properties/.

switch.name: TARGET_TEMP
switch.description: Contains the temperature (in Kelvin) of wall element (iwall) at depth layer (idepth). If plate_option.eq.1, will be set to backplate_temp(iwall). If plate_option.eq.2 and empty, will be set to equilibrium 1-D profile deduced from plasma incoming fluxes. If plate-option.eq.3, must be set.

switch.name: INERTIAL_COOLING
switch.description: Indicates whether wall element (iwall) is inertially cooled instead of actively cooled.

switch.name: BACKPLATE_TEMP
switch.description: Contains the temperature (in Kelvin) maintained by cooling at the back end of wall element (iwall).

switch.name: PLATE_THICKNESS
switch.description: Contains the thickness (in meters) of wall element (iwall).

switch.name: COATING_THICKNESS
switch.description: Contains the thickness (in meters) of the eventual coating on wall element (iwall).

switch.name: PLATE_TIME_FACTOR
switch.description: Multiplier to the time for the equations for temperature and composition evolution of wall element (iwall).

switch.name: DEPOSITION
switch.description: Contains the amount of eroded material (in atoms) of species (itrack) from wall element(iwall).

switch.name: EROSION
switch.description: Contains the amount of eroded material (in atoms) of species (itrack) from wall element(iwall).

switch.name: CHEMICAL_SPUTTERING
switch.description: Contains the chemical sputter yield of 'sput_dst' or 'sput_dst_bulk' species on wall element (iwall) caused by B2 species (is).

switch.name: PHYSICAL_SPUTTERING
switch.description: Contains the physical sputter yield of 'sput_dst' or 'sput_dst_bulk' species on wall element (iwall) caused by B2 species (is).

switch.name: PHYSICAL_SPUTTERING_ENERGY
switch.description: Contains the fraction of returned energy carried by sputtered particles of species 'sput_dst' or 'sput_dst_bulk' species from wall element (iwall) caused by B2 species (is).

switch.name: RES_SPUTTERING
switch.description: Contains the RES sputter yield of 'sput_dst' or 'sput_dst_bulk' species on wall element (iwall) caused by B2 species (is).

switch.name: THERMAL_EVAPORATION
switch.description: Contains the rate of thermal evaporation of species (is) (in particles/second) from wall element (iwall).

switch.name: BACKSCATTERING
switch.description: Contains the backscattering fraction for incoming B2 species (is) onto wall element (iwall).

switch.name: BACKSCATTERING_ENERGY
switch.description: Contains the backscattered energy fraction for incoming B2 species (is) onto wall element (iwall).

switch.name: PLATE_TIME
switch.description: Indicates how much simulation time has elapsed for wall element (iwall) (in seconds).

switch.name: PLATE_AREA
switch.description: Indicates the wall area (in square meters) for wall element (iwall). Defaults to the area computed from the B2 grid information.

switch.name: MONOLAYER_DEPOSITION
switch.description: Contains the amount of deposited material (in monolayers) from species (itrack) onto wall element (iwall).

switch.name: MONOLAYER_EROSION
switch.description: Contains the amount of eroded material (in monolayers) of species (itrack) from wall element (iwall).

switch.name: LAYER_NCONSTITUENTS
switch.description: Indicates the number of elemental constituents within the surface layer of wall element (NWALL).

switch.name: LAYER_NZCONSTITUENTS
switch.description: Contains the atomic numbers Z of the various elements present within the surface layer of wall element (iwall). Defaults to 6 for the first value, 0 otherwise.

switch.name: LAYER_NRELCONSTITUENTS
switch.description: Contains the relative atomic abundances of the various elements present within the surface layer of wall element (iwall). Defaults to 1.0 for the first value, 0.0 otherwise.

switch.name: EXP
switch.description: Name of the experiment being modelled.

switch.name: SHOT
switch.description: Shot number identifying the run. Defaults to the last number found in shotnumber.history, or 0 if the file is not found.

switch.name: TIME
switch.description: Time point of the experimental shot being simulated.

switch.name: COMMENT
switch.description: Label for the run.

switch.name: TIMEDEP
switch.description: saves data from b2time.nc.

switch.name: SNAPSHOT
switch.description: saves data from b2fplasma.

switch.name: TALLIES
switch.description: saves data from b2tallies.nc.

switch.name: MOVIES
switch.description: saves data from b2movies.nc.

switch.name: OVERWRITE_SHOTNUMBER
switch.description: Indicate the shot number to overwrite (to be used only when updating an already saved run with 'resave_mds' script). Defaults to the last number found in shotnumber.history, or 0 if the file is not found.

switch.name: NBC
switch.description: Number of boundary segments.

switch.name: BCCHAR
switch.description: specifying the nature of the boundary segment N "North" boundary S "South" boundary W "West" boundary E "East" boundary X "X" boundary used for specifying a fixed value on a row of cells Y "Y" boundary used for specifying a fixed value on a column of cells

switch.name: CONPAR
switch.description: supplies the additional parameters needed for the option specified by BCCON

switch.name: MOMPAR
switch.description: supplies the additional parameters needed for the option specified by BCMOM

switch.name: ENEPAR
switch.description: supplies the additional parameters needed for the option specified by BCENE

switch.name: ENIPAR
switch.description: supplies the additional parameters needed for the option specified by BCENI

switch.name: POTPAR
switch.description: Contains parameters helping to define the boundary conditions for the potential equation. See description of BCPOT below for details.

switch.name: BCPOS
switch.description: for North, South or X boundary conditions, it specifies the row index; for West, East and Y boundary conditions, it specifies the column index

switch.name: BCSTART
switch.description: for North, South or X boundary conditions, it specifies the start column index; for West, East and Y boundary conditions, it specifies the start row index

switch.name: BCEND
switch.description: North, South or X boundary conditions, it specifies the end column index; for West, East and Y boundary conditions, it specifies the end row index

switch.name: BC_LIST_SIZE
switch.description: Contains the size of the list of cells where a boundary condition is applied.

switch.name: BC_LIST_X
switch.description: Contains the X-coordinate of the cells where boundaries conditions are applied.

switch.name: BC_LIST_Y
switch.description: Contains the Y-coordinate of the cells where boundaries conditions are applied.

switch.name: BCCON
switch.description: specifying the type of density boundary condition for each segment and species (fastest varying index is species); makes use of CONPAR to specify additional information, as indicated 1 prescribe the value of the density, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the required density in m^-3 2 prescribe the gradient of the density, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the required density gradient in m^-4 3 sheath conditions, CONPAR(,,1) not used (zero gradient is used) 4 prescribe the value of the density, weakly a mixed boundary condition, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the required density in m^-3 and CONPAR(,,2) specifies the "strength" of the boundary condition 5 prescribe the particle flux per unit area, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the required particle flux density in m^-2 s^-1 6 prescribe the total particle flux for a constant density, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the particle flux in s -1 7 prescribe the density as a function of other plasma parameters [not yet available] 8 prescribe the total particle flux with constant flux density, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the particle flux in s -1 9 prescribe the decay length for the density, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the gradient length in m 10 leakage option for density, recommended for cases with drifts, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the leakage factor, ² in Γ loss = ²C s,a n a 11 particle flux feedback boundary condition, CONPAR(,,1) not used, derived from CBSNA(0,IS,IREG). The species used must be declared using the b2stbc isfeedback switch. 12 particle density feedback boundary condition, as above, CONPAR(,,1) not used, derived from CBSNA(0,IS,IREG). The species used must be declared using the b2stbc isfeedback switch. 13 particle density to achieve specified total flux, CONPAR(,,1) is the specified radial flux crossing the surface b2stbc type13 ref steps away from the boundary, to which is added the returning flux of ionised neutrals that crossed into the plasma core (via CONPAR(,,3)) when running with Eirene and the 'ionising core' switch is used, CONPAR(,,2) is the strength of the feedback. 14 sound speed velocity flux, CONPAR(,,1) is a multiplier to the outgoing sound speed C s, collective . 15 prescribe a radial leakage velocity, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the leakage velocity in units of the local thermal velocity. 16 particle density to achieve specified total flux, used with ASTRA coupling. The total desired flux is summed over all BCCON=16 core boundaries. CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired particle flux in s -1 . 18 prescribe total main ion particle flux, used with ASTRA coupling. 19 particle flux feedback boundary condition, flux is summed over neutrals and ions, for coupling with ASTRA. The total desired flux is summed over all BCCON=19 core boundaries. This boundary condition applies to ions in their highest ionisation stage. CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired particle flux in s -1 . 20 constant density feedback condition, CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired density in m -3 . 21 prescribe the value of the density and add a density perturbation to get a solution which is as close as possible to neoclassical theory. It is recommended to use this boundary condition together with corresponding condition on ion temperature (BCENI=24). The total desired density is summed over all BCCON=21 core boundaries. CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired density in m -3 . 22 Feedback boundary condition: given total particle flux with constant average density. The poloidal density variation is added to make the solution as close as possible to the neoclassical value derived for pure H/D/T plasma. It is recommended to use this boundary condition with corresponding boundary condition on ion temperature (BCENI=23,24). The total desired flux is summed over all BCCON=22 core boundaries. CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired particle flux in s -1 . 23 Feedback boundary condition: given sum of integrated neutrals and main ion particle fluxes with constant average density. The poloidal density variation is added to make the solution as close as possible to the neoclassical value derived for pure H/D/T plasma. It is recommended to use this boundary condition with corresponding boundary condition on ion temperature (BCENI=23,24). The total desired flux is summed over all BCCON=23 core boundaries. CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired particle flux in s -1 . 24 constant density feedback scaled by density on the ring bc type21 ref away CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired density in m -3 . CONPAR(,,2) is the strength of the feedback 25 Feedback boundary condition: prescribe the average value of the density and add a density perturbation from neighbouring radial cell. This boundary condition is suitable for any species of a multi-species plasma (i.e. in the case when the condition used in BCCON=21 fails). It is recommended to use this boundary condition together with the corresponding condition on ion temperature (BCENI=26,27). CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired average density in m -3 . 26 Feedback boundary condition: prescribe the total ion flux and find the average density. A density perturbation is taken from the neighbouring radial cell. This boundary condition is suitable for any species of a multi-species plasma (i.e. in the case when the condition used in BCCON=22 fails). It is recommended to use this boundary condition together with a corresponding condition on the ion temperature (BCENI=26,27). The total desired flux is summed over all BCCON=26 core boundaries. CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired particle flux in s -1 . 27 Feedback boundary condition: prescribe the particle flux sum for all neutrals and ions belonging to a given isonuclear sequence and find the average density of the highest ionization stage. A density perturbation is taken from the neighbouring radial cell. It is recommended to use this boundary condition together with corresponding condition on ion temperature (BCENI=25,26). The total desired flux is summed over all BCCON=27 core boundaries. CONPAR(,,1) specifies the desired particle flux in s -1 .

switch.name: BCMOM
switch.description: specifying the type of parallel momentum or velocity boundary condition for each segment and species (fastest varying index is species); makes use of MOMPAR to specify additional information, as indicated 1 prescribe the value of the parallel velocity, MOMPAR(,,1) specifies the parallel velocity in m.s^-1 2 prescribe the gradient of the parallel velocity, MOMPAR(,,1) specifies the parallel velocity gradient in s -1 3 sheath conditions, mach number as input, if MOMPAR(,,2) < 2 1 , then the velocity is set to exactly MOMPAR(,,1)*C s, collective , otherwise the velocity is set to be at least MOMPAR(,,1) *C s, species 4 prescribe the value of the velocity, weakly a mixed boundary condition, MOMPAR(,,1) specifies the parallel velocity in m.s -1 and MOMPAR(,,2) specifies the "strength" of the boundary condition 5 prescribe the parallel momentum flux per unit area, MOMPAR(,,1) specifies the parallel momentum flux density in N.m -2 6 prescribe the total parallel momentum flux for a constant parallel velocity [not yet available] 7 prescribe the parallel momentum as a function of other plasma parameters [not yet available] 8 special : limited shear, imposes zero gradient for the Mach number. [[[Eventually intended to have MOMPAR(,,1) specify the gradient of the Mach number in m -1 ]]] 9 prescribe the total parallel momentum flux with constant flux density, MOMPAR(,,1) specifies the parallel momentum flux in N 10 prescribe the decay length for the parallel momentum, MOMPAR(,,1) specifies the decay length in m 11 Rozhansky viscosity condition for the parallel momentum, MOMPAR(,,1) is not used 12 Condition from b2stbc spb for the parallel momentum 13 sheath boundary condition from b2stbc spb for the parallel momentum 14 Condition from b2stbc spb for the parallel momentum 15 Prescribe the value of the parallel velocity, scaled with $lt;B$gt;/B 16 Prescribe the average value of the parallel velocity MOMPAR(,,1) specifies the parallelv elocity in m.s -1 17 leakage option for parallel momentum, MOMPAR(,,1) specifies the leakage factor, ² in Γ loss = ²C s,a m a n a u a

switch.name: BCENE
switch.description: specifying the type of electron energy or temperature boundary condition for each segment; makes use of ENEPAR to specify additional information, as indicated 1 prescribe the value of the electron temperature, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the temperature in eV 2 prescribe the gradient of the electron temperature, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the temperature gradient in eV.m -1 3 sheath conditions, electron energy transmission, ENEPAR(,1) specifies an additional contribution to the energy transmission coefficient in addition to that of the potential difference [the sound speed used depends on settings of MOMPAR(,ISMAIN,2) and the b2stbc sound model switch] 4 prescribe the value of the electron temperature, weakly a mixed boundary condition, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the temperature in eV and ENEPAR(,2) specifies the "strength" of the boundary condition 5 prescribe the electron energy flux per unit area, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux density in W.m -2 6 prescribe the total electron energy flux for a constant electron temperature, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux in W (no longer supported, use types [16] or [17] instead) 7 prescribe the electron temperature as a function of other plasma parameters [not yet available] 8 prescribe the total electron heat flux with constant flux density, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux in W 9 prescribe the decay length for the electron temperature, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the decay length in m (can also use type [19] instead) 10 feedback option for core, ENEPAR(,1) not used, derived from cbshe(0,coreregno) 11 not used 12 sheath conditions, electron energy transmission coefficient, ENEPAR(,1) specifies an energy transmission factor, δ e in Q e = δ e Γ e T e 13 prescribe the electron energy flux per unit area proportional to temperature, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux density per temperature in W.m -2 .J -1 (the temperature here in J) 14 leakage option for electron energy, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the leakage factor, ² in Γ loss = ²C s, collective n e T e 15 not used 16 Feedback boundary condition with constant temperature, ENEPAR(,1) specifies the power flux in W across the flux surface with index b2stbc type16 ref (default=-1), ENEPAR(,2) should be something like 0.1 and specifies the strength of the feedback. Also see type [17] below. Available if bcene 16 style=0 (default). If bcene 16 style=1, integrated electron heat flux with constant T e , summed over all core boundaries with BCENE=16. 17 Feedback boundary condition with constant shared temperature for both electrons and ions, with ENEPAR(,1) + ENIPAR(,1) giving the total power flux in W across the flux surface with index b2stbc type16 ref (default=-1), ENEPAR(,2) should be something like 0.1 and specifies the strength of the feedback. Replaces [16] for high densities and large values of b2stbc type16 ref. 18 Fractional drop condition. Not yet working. 19 same as [9] but to be used when simultaneously setting BCCON=1 on the same boundary. 20 Feedback boundary condition with constant temperature, as per type [16] but with type20 switches and a different feedback scheme. 21 from b2stbc spb 22 radial leakage condition for the electron temperature. ENEPAR(,1) specifies the leakage velocity in units of the electron thermal velocity. A temperature gradient such that the diffusive flux is set to match this leakage is imposed.

switch.name: BCENI
switch.description: specifying the type of ion energy or temperature boundary condition for each segment; makes use of ENIPAR to specify additional information, as indicated 1 prescribe the value of the ion temperature, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the temperature in eV 2 prescribe the gradient of the ion temperature, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the temperature gradient in eV.m -1 3 sheath conditions, ion energy transmission, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the contribution to the energy transmission coefficient [the sound speed used depends on settings of MOMPAR(,ISMAIN,2) and the b2stbc sound model switch] 4 prescribe the value of the ion temperature, weakly a mixed boundary condition, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the temperature in eV and ENIPAR(,2) specifies the "strength" of the boundary condition 5 prescribe the ion energy flux per unit area, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux density in W.m -2 6 prescribe the total ion energy flux for a constant ion temperature, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux in W (no longer supported, use types [16] or [17] instead) 7 prescribe the ion temperature as a function of other plasma parameters [not yet available] 8 prescribe the total ion heat flux with constant flux density, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux in W 9 prescribe the decay length for the ion temperature, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the decay length in m (can also use type [19] instead) 10 feedback option for core, ENIPAR(,1) not used, derived from cbshi(0,ISMAIN,coreregno) 11 sheath conditions, ion P energy transmission coefficient, ENIPAR(,1) specifies an energy transmission factor, δ i in Q i = δ i T i a n a C s,a 12 sheath conditions, ion P energy transmission coefficient, ENIPAR(,1) specifies an energy transmission factor, δ i in Q i = δ i T i a Γ a 13 prescribe the ion energy flux per unit area proportional to temperature, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux density per temperature in W.m -2 .J -1 (the temperature here in J) 14 leakage option for ion energy, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the leakage factor, ² in Γ loss = ²C s, collective n i T i 15 not used 16 Feedback boundary condition with constant temperature, ENIPAR(,1) specifies the power flux in W across the flux surface with index b2stbc type16 ref (default=-1), ENIPAR(,2) should be something like 0.1 and specifies the strength of the feedback. Also see type [17] below. Available if bceni 16 style=0 (default). If bceni 16 style=1, integrated ion heat flux with constant T i , summed over all core boundaries with BCENI=16. 17 Feedback boundary condition with constant shared temperature for both electrons and ions, see BCENE=17 above for description. 18 Fractional drop condition. Not yet working. 19 same as [9] but to be used when simultaneously setting BCCON=1 on the same boundary. 20 Feedback boundary condition with constant temperature, as per type [16] but with type20 switches and a different feedback scheme. 21 from b2stbc spb 22 Radial leakage velocity for the ion temperature. ENIPAR(,1) specifies the leakage velocity in units of the collective ion thermal velocity. A temperature gradient such that the diffusive flux is set to match this leakage is imposed. 23 Prescribe the poloidally averaged value of the ion temperature and introduce a poloidal variation as close as possible to neoclassical solution. It is recommended to use this boundary condition together with corresponding condition on ion density (BCCON=21,22,23). The average is taken over all core boundaries with BCENI=23. ENIPAR(,1) specifies the temperature in eV 24 Feedback boundary condition with prescribed total ion flux, constant poloidally averaged ion temperature and a poloidal variation as close as possible to neoclassical solution. It is recommended to use this boundary condition together with corresponding condition on ion density (BCCON=21,22,23). The flux is summed over all core boundaries with BCENI=24. ENIPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux in W 25 Constant temperature feedback scaled by temperature on the ring bc type21 ref away ENIPAR(,,1) specifies the desired ion temperature in eV . ENIPAR(,,2) is the strength of the feedback 26 Prescribe the poloidally averaged value of the ion temperature and introduce a poloidal variation in a simplified manner. This boundary condition is suitable for any plasma composition (i.e. when BCENI=23 fails). It is recommended to use this boundary condition together with corresponding condition on ion density (BCCON = 25,26,27). The average is taken over all core boundaries with BCENI=26. ENIPAR(,1) specifies the temperature in eV 27 Feedback boundary condition with prescribed total ion heat flux, constant poloidally averaged ion temperature and a poloidal variation in a simplified manner. This boundary condition is suitable for any plasma composition (i.e. when BCENI=24 fails). It is recommended to use this boundary condition together with corresponding condition on ion density (BCCON = 25,26,27). The flux is summed over all core boundaries with BCENI=27. ENIPAR(,1) specifies the energy flux in W

switch.name: BCPOT
switch.description: specifying the type of electric potential or current boundary condition for each segment; makes use of POTPAR to specify additional information, as indicated 1 prescribe the value of the potential, POTPAR(,1) specifies the potential in V 2 prescribe the gradient of the potential, POTPAR(,1) specifies the potential gradient in V.m -1 3 sheath conditions, POTPAR(,2) used for biasing [see code for details] 4 prescribe the value of the potential weakly a mixed boundary condition, POTPAR(,1) specifies the potential in V and POTPAR(,2) specifies the "strength" of the boundary condition 5 prescribe the current flux density per unit area, POTPAR(,1) specifies the electric current flux density in A.m -2 6 prescribe the total current flux density for a constant potential [not yet available] 7 prescribe the potential as a function of other plasma parameters [not yet available] 8 prescribe the total electric current with constant flux density, POTPAR(,1) specifies the electric current in A 9 prescribe the decay length for the potential, POTPAR(,1) specifies the decay length in m 10 feedback option for core [not yet tested!!!!!!!!!] (based on using cbsch(0,coreregno)) 11 sheath conditions, electron energy transmission from b2stbc spb POTPAR(,2) specifies the bias potential in V 12 Imposes the currents due to drifts for the South core boundary. Must be used in conjunction with the switch setting istyle cur contr on S and N=2 13 Imposes the currents due to drifts for the South private flux and North boundaries. Must be used in conjunction with istyle cur contr on S and N=2 16 Feedback condition of constant electric potential for a given prescribed current. The prescribed current (in A) is given by the sum of the POTPAR(,1) of all the boundary conditions with BCPOT=16. Still experimental, will not work for drift cases. 21 constant potential feedback scaled by potential on the ring bc type21 ref away, POTPAR(,,1) specifies the desired electric potential in V , POTPAR(,,2) is the strength of the feedback.

switch.name: GAMMAI
switch.description: ratio of specific heats (adiabatic coefficient)

switch.name: GAMMAE
switch.description: secondary electron emission coefficient

switch.name: LBNDUSR
switch.description: If .true. will also call Bas' boundary condition routine after the end of the physics boundary condition routine (governed by the data from b2ah.dat and b2mn.dat).

switch.name: LFEEDBACK
switch.description: Indicates whether a feedback scheme is used. Obsolete. Superceded by 'b2stbc_feedback'

switch.name: NNISO
switch.description: number of dead (or isolated) regions

switch.name: NIISO
switch.description: Density of species (is) in (m-3) to impose in isolated regions.

switch.name: TEISO
switch.description: Electron temperature (in eV) to impose in isolated regions.

switch.name: TIISO
switch.description: Ion temperature (in eV) to impose in isolated regions.

switch.name: PHIISO
switch.description: Electric potential (in V) to impose in isolated regions.

switch.name: NXISO1
switch.description: Column number of bottom left corner of the dead region (iiso)

switch.name: NXISO2
switch.description: Column number of top right corner of the dead region (iiso).

switch.name: NYISO1
switch.description: integer array of size NNISO. Row number of bottom left corner of the dead region (iiso)

switch.name: NYISO2
switch.description: Row number of top right corner of the dead region (iiso)

switch.name: BOUNDARY_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /BOUNDARY/ namelist.

switch.name: BOUNDARY_TIME_MOD
switch.description: When the B2 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(BOUNDARY_TIME_MOD), exceeds BOUNDARY_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from BOUNDARY_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist as the ELM count (here time/BOUNDARY_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if BOUNDARY_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: BOUNDARY_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: Time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle after which a new /BOUNDARY/ namelist is read.

switch.name: LCBS
switch.description: Indices of the core boundary segments in B2 for passing to EIRENE. Defaults to the list of 'S' boundaries in regions 1 and 5.

switch.name: WRITE_NML_BND
switch.description: If .true. (default), writes the content of the namelist to stdout after it has been read.

switch.name: SAVED_CBSHE_CORE
switch.description: Last value used for the core electron energy radial flux feedback.

switch.name: SAVED_CBSHI_CORE
switch.description: Last value used for the core ion energy radial flux feedback.

switch.name: SAVED_CBSNA_CORE
switch.description: Last value used for the core particle radial flux feedback. Corresponds to 'isfeedback' B2 species.

switch.name: SAVED_CBSCH_CORE
switch.description: Last value used for the core radial electric current feedback.

switch.name: SAVED_CBSNA_PFR1
switch.description: Last value used for the radial inner PFR boundary flux feedback. Corresponds to 'isfeedback' B2 species.

switch.name: SAVED_CBSNA_PFR2
switch.description: Last value used for the radial outer PFR boundary flux feedback. Corresponds to 'isfeedback' B2 species.

switch.name: SAVED_CBSNA_SOL
switch.description: Last value used for the radial particle flux feedback in the SOL. Corresponds to 'isfeedback' B2 species.

switch.name: VACUUM_COMMUNICATION
switch.description: If > 0, allows for a communication of particle fluxes across vacuum regions. This option only applies to neutrals. The density boundary condition is based on the difference between the average pressure and the local pressure.

switch.name: VACUUM_COMMUNICATION_NREG
switch.description: Number of communicating vacuum regions.

switch.name: VACUUM_COMMUNICATION_METHOD
switch.description: Option for resorbing the pressure difference. 1: Try to set a flux. Corr = (beta*ave_pressure - pressure)/temp * alpha 2: Try to set a density based on pressure equality. Corr = alpha * beta * pressure / Ti

switch.name: VACUUM_COMMUNICATION_IY
switch.description: Radial index of the ring on which the neutral pressure is computed for region IREG.

switch.name: VACUUM_COMMUNICATION_IX1
switch.description: Poloidal lower bound of the range over which the neutral pressure is computed for region IREG.

switch.name: VACUUM_COMMUNICATION_IX2
switch.description: Poloidal upper bound of the range over which the neutral pressure is computed for region IREG.

switch.name: VACUUM_COMMUNICATION_ALPHA
switch.description: Parameter for setting the pressure correction. See above.

switch.name: VACUUM_COMMINICATION_BETA
switch.description: Parameter for setting the pressure correction. See above.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_TARGET
switch.description: Sets the target density of species (ISPECIES) for the feedback scheme.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_TIME
switch.description: Sets the time of reference (in s) for the feedback of species (ISPECIES).

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_CHOICE
switch.description: Choice of quantity on which the feedback is computed: 0: no action 1: local species density (averaged over the rectangle of cells[IX1:IX2,IY1:IY2]). Summed over all charge states of that species. 2: local electron density (averaged over the rectangle of cells[IX1:IX2,IY1:IY2]). 3: outer midplane separatrix electron density. 4: total particle content for that species. 5: total ion content for that species (not including neutrals). 6: neutral particle flux through the core boundary. 7: relative average concentration of this species at the separatrix.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_OPTION
switch.description: Option for computing the new fedback quantity. 0: no action 1: rescale slowed by na_feedback_alpha 2: pure rescale 3: rescaling slowed by tanh_log 4: rescale done according to SOLPS4 formula The target waveform for the particle content is N = C + V*(time-T) and the current puffing rate S is adjusted S --> max(0, min(X,S + D)), where D = F*((N - <N>)/dt + (<N>_prev - >N<)/dt_prev) 5: rescale done according to SOLPS4 formula: N = C*exp((time-T)*V) 6: rescale slowed by na_feedback_alpha (SOLPS4 style)

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_ACTUATOR
switch.description: Choice for the actuator used for the feedback. 0: no action 1: gas puff via boundary condition 2: rescale na 3: core boundary flux condition

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_ALPHA
switch.description: Factor by which the rescaling is slowed. Rescaling factor is : Option 1: (1 + alpha*target/current) / (1 + alpha) Option 3: 2**(tanh(log(x)/beta)*log(alpha)/log(2)) Options 4 ' 5: Corresponds to parameter F

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_BETA
switch.description: Factor by which the rescaling is slowed. See above. Options 4 ' 5: Corresponds to parameter V (ffb_rtvn)

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_CONST
switch.description: Options 4 ' 5: Corresponds to parameter C. If negative, C is computed as the initial total particle content of the sequence.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_IX1
switch.description: Lower poloidal bound for the region of which the density is being averaged.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_IX2
switch.description: Upper poloidal bound for the region of which the density is being averaged.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_IY1
switch.description: Lower radial bound for the region of which the density is being averaged.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_IY2
switch.description: Upper poloidal bound for the region of which the density is being averaged.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_IB
switch.description: Index of boundary condition through which the feedback is being applied.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_PUFF_MIN
switch.description: Minimum gas puff being applied.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_PUFF_MAX
switch.description: Maximum gas puff being applied.

switch.name: NA_FEEDBACK_OVERSHOOT
switch.description: If the density is larger than target*overshoot, the gas puff is turned off.

switch.name: NSDATA
switch.description: Number of points over which the source profile of (kind_data,kind_source,is) is defined. Should not exceed NY+2. If KIND_DATA=1, the data is expressed as a profile in physical distance from the separatrix (in metres) along the outer midplane. The user can change this default reference location by use of the 'set_transport_i[xy]ref' switches. If KIND_DATA=2, the data is expressed as a profile in flux distance from the separatrix (not yet available).

switch.name: SDATA
switch.description: For (i,ir,ik,is) in (1:2,1:NY+2,1:NKIND_SOURCE,0:NS), SDATA(1,ir,:,:) contains the radial location of the profile point (ir). SDATA(2,ir,:,:) contains the source profile value at point (ir). KIND_SOURCE=1 means particle source of species (is) (in particles/m3) KIND_SOURCE=2 means parallel momentum source for species (is) (in kg.m/s/m3) KIND_SOURCE=3 means electron heat source (in Watts/m3) KIND_SOURCE=4 means ion heat source (in Watts/m3) KIND_SOURCE=5 means electric charge source (in Coulombs/m3) KIND_SOURCE=6 means non-ambipolar electron particle source (in e/m3)

switch.name: NXDATA
switch.description: Number of points over which the axial profile of (kind_data,kind_source,is) is defined. Should not exceed NX+2. Defaults to 0. If KIND_DATA=1, the data is expressed as a profile in physical distance, here connection length, rescaled from 0.0 to 1.0. For closed field lines, the reference location for the zero of distance is set by use of the 'set_transport_i[xy]ref' switches. If KIND_DATA=2, the data is expressed as a profile in (ix) cell index, again normalized from 0.0 to 1.0 to match the [0:nx-1] interval.

switch.name: XDATA
switch.description: Multiplier to the poloidal source profile in the axial direction. Same convention for KIND_SOURCE as above.

switch.name: DIVHEAT
switch.description: Additional divertor ion heat source (in Watts/m3)

switch.name: SOURCES_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /PROFILE/ namelist. Quantities not present in the new file will be inherited from the old one.

switch.name: SOURCES_TIME_MOD
switch.description: When the B2 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(SOURCES_TIME_MOD),exceeds SOURCES_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from SOURCES_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist if the ELM count (here time/SOURCES_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if SOURCES_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: SOURCES_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: Time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle after which a new /PROFILE/ namelist is read.

switch.name: NDATA
switch.description: Number of points over which the source profile of (kind_data,kind_coef,is) is defined. Should not exceed NY+2. If KIND_DATA=1, the data is expressed as a profiles in physical distance from the separatrix (in metres). If KIND_DATA=2, the data is expressed as a profile in flux distance from the separatrix (not yet available).

switch.name: TDATA
switch.description: For (i,ir,ik,is) in (1:3,1:NY+2,1:NKIND_COEFF,0:NS), TDATA(1,ir,:,:) contains the radial location of the profile point (ir). By default, these are measured at the outer midplane, as distance to the separatrix in metres. The user can change this by setting the 'set_transport_i[xy]ref' switches to choose a different location for the distance reference. TDATA(2,ir,:,:) contains the transport profile value at point (ir). TDATA(3,ir,:,:) contains the ELM transport profile value at point (ir). KIND_COEFF=1 means density-driven particle diffusivity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=2 means pressure-driven particle diffusivity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=3 means ion heat diffusivity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=4 means electron heat diffusivity KIND_COEFF=5 means poloidal pinch velocity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=6 means radial pinch velocity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=7 means viscosity for species (is) KIND_COEFF=8 means field-driven radial current conductivity KIND_COEFF=9 means temperature-driven radial current conductivity

switch.name: ADDSPEC
switch.description: ADDSPEC(is,ikind,spec).ge.0, then the transport coefficient profile of type (ikind) from species (spec) is also used for species with index ADDSPEC(is,ikind,spec).

switch.name: TRANSPORT_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /TRANSPORT/ namelist.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD
switch.description: When the B2 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD),exceeds TRANSPORT_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from TRANSPORT_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist if the ELM count (here time/TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: Time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle after which a new /TRANSPORT/ namelist is read.

switch.name: REGION_FLAGS
switch.description: If region_flags(ireg,ikind) is .true. (default), then the transport parameters profiles for region (ireg) and kind (ikind) are used.

switch.name: NO_PFLUX
switch.description: If .true., transport coefficients profiles are not implemented in the private flux regions.

switch.name: POLOIDAL_SCALING
switch.description: If .true., then the transport coefficients profiles from the current b2.transport.inputfile are increased by a factor of 1.0+Gaussian where Gaussian is a Gaussian profile in the poloidal direction of amplitude SCALING_STRENGTH extending from SCALING_IX_BEGIN to SCALING_IX_END inclusively. The profile has a decay length of SCALING_WIDTH (in units of the number of poloidal cells).

switch.name: SCALING_STRENGTH
switch.description: See above.

switch.name: SCALING_WIDTH
switch.description: See above. Defaults to about 1/3 of the interval over which the scaling is to be done.

switch.name: SCALING_IX_BEGIN
switch.description: See above.

switch.name: SCALING_IX_END
switch.description: See above.

switch.name: ELM_TIME_BEGIN
switch.description: Time (in seconds) modulo ELM_TIME_PERIOD at which the ELM phase begins and the ELM data must be used.

switch.name: ELM_TIME_END
switch.description: Time (in seconds) modulo ELM_TIME_PERIOD at which the ELM phase ends and the ELM data is no longer used.

switch.name: ELM_TIME_PERIOD
switch.description: Indicates the real frequency of simulated ELMs. See above for usage. If zero, no ELM profiles are used.

switch.name: ELM_IX_BEGIN
switch.description: Poloidal position at which the ELM profile starts to be applied.

switch.name: ELM_IX_END
switch.description: Poloidal position at which the ELM profile ends being applied.

switch.name: SAVED_VOLREC
switch.description: Contains the last value of the strength of volume recombination sources from stratum (istra).

switch.name: DTCO
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the continuity equation of species (is) in region (ireg).

switch.name: DTMO
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the parallel momentum equation of species (is) in region (ireg).

switch.name: DTEE
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the electron heat equation in region (ireg).

switch.name: DTEI
switch.description: Multiplier to the time used in solving the ion heat equation in region (ireg).

switch.name: SOLVECO
switch.description: Indicates whether the continuity equation for species (is) is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEMO
switch.description: Indicates whether the parallel momentum equation for species (is) is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEPO
switch.description: Indicates whether the electron energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEEE
switch.description: Indicates whether the electron energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: SOLVEEI
switch.description: Indicates whether the ion energy equation is to be solved in region (ireg). Subservient to 'no_solve' switch.

switch.name: TIME_FACTOR_REQUIRED
switch.description: Minimum time scale of evolution allowed for all equations. Only active is 'b2srsm_enable' is set to 1.

switch.name: CORE_DT_SUPPRESSION
switch.description: De-multiplier to the timestep in the core. Only active if less than 1. Should be larger than 0. Applies fully to the innermost core ring of cells (IY .eq. -1). See CORE_DT_FACTOR for further use.

switch.name: CORE_DT_FACTOR
switch.description: Multiplier to the timestep in the core. Only active is less than 1. Should be larger than 0. Multiplies each successive core ring of cells (increasing IY) by CORE_DT_FACTOR, until the local time step multiplier is equal to 1.

switch.name: NUMERICS_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /NUMERICS/ namelist.

switch.name: NUMERICS_TIME_MOD
switch.description: When the B2 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(NUMERICS_TIME_MOD), exceeds NUMERICS_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from NUMERICS_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist as the ELM count (here time/NUMERICS_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if NUMERICS_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: NUMERICS_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: Time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle after which a new /NUMERICS/ namelist is read.

switch.name: WRITE_NML_NUM
switch.description: If .true. (default), writes the content of the namelist to stdout after it has been read.

switch.name: ETA_HCE_MULT
switch.description: Used by the user specified set_transport_eta transport model. See code for details.

switchgroup.name: FLAG*
switchgroup.description: All flags follow: FLAG=0: Use model from b2ah.dat and b2mn.dat. Default. FLAG=1: Constant, value set to PARM. FLAG=2: 1/N model, multiplied by PARM. FLAG=3: Bohm model, multiplied by PARM. FLAG=4: flux-scaled model, multiplied by PARM.

switch.name: FLAG_DNA
switch.description: Transport model flag for density-driven diffusion.

switch.name: FLAG_DPA
switch.description: Transport model flag for pressure-driven dffusion.

switch.name: FLAG_VLA
switch.description: Transport model flag for pinch velocity.

switch.name: FLAG_VSA
switch.description: Transport model flag for viscosity.

switch.name: FLAG_HCI
switch.description: Transport model flag for ion heat diffusivity.

switch.name: FLAG_HCE
switch.description: Transport model flag for electron heat diffusivity.

switch.name: FLAG_SIG
switch.description: Transport model flag for field-driven current radial conductivity.

switch.name: FLAG_ALF
switch.description: Transport model flag for temperature-driven current radial conductivity.

switch.name: PARM_DNA
switch.description: Parameter for the density-driven particle diffusion coefficient for species (is).

switch.name: PARM_DPA
switch.description: Parameter for the pressure-driven particle diffusion coefficient for species (is).

switch.name: PARM_VLA
switch.description: Parameter for the anomalous radial pinch velocity for species (is).

switch.name: PARM_HCI
switch.description: Parameter for the heat diffusivity coefficient for species (is).real*8

switch.name: PARM_HCE
switch.description: Parameter for the electron heat diffusivity coefficient.

switch.name: PARM_SIG
switch.description: Parameter for the anomalous radial field-driven current conductivity.

switch.name: PARM_ALF
switch.description: Parameter for the anomalous radial temperature-driven current conductivity.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_FILENAME
switch.description: Name of the next file to use for reading a new /TRANSPORT/ namelist.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD
switch.description: When the B2 run simulation time, in seconds, modulo(TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD), exceeds TRANSPORT_TIME_SWITCH, reads the new namelist from TRANSPORT_FILENAME. Also switches to the new namelist if the ELM count (here time/TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD) changes. Only active if TRANSPORT_TIME_MOD is greater than 0.

switch.name: TRANSPORT_TIME_SWITCH
switch.description: Time (in seconds) within an ELM cycle after which a new /TRANSPORT/ namelist is read.

switchgroup.name: CFLM*
switchgroup.description: For all flux limit multipliers, setting them to zero turns off the corresponding flux limiter.

switch.name: CFLME
switch.description: Multiplier to the electron heat flux limit.

switch.name: CFLMI
switch.description: Multiplier to the ion heat flux limit.

switch.name: CFLMV
switch.description: Multiplier to the viscous heat flux limit.

switch.name: WRITE_NML_TRANSP
switch.description: If .true. (default), writes the content of the namelist to stdout after it has been read.

switchgroup.name: *_CNV
switchgroup.description: The following four parameters are only activated if 'b2tqna_user_transport' is set to '6'.

switch.name: VOUT_CNV
switch.description: Value of the "blob" convection velocity for species (is) at the outer grid edge (in m/s).

switch.name: PW0_CNV
switch.description: Exponent in radial profile of the "blob" velocity for species (is).

switch.name: PW1_CNV
switch.description: First exponent in poloidal profile of the "blob" velocity for species (is).

switch.name: PW2_CNV
switch.description: Second exponent in a poloidal profile of the "blob" velocity for species (is).

switch.name: LHETRGTS
switch.default: -2 for the first element, -3 for the second element and 0 otherwise
switch.description: List of surface indices (EIRENE notation) which are used for calculation of helium enrichment.

switch.name: LPFRB_I
switch.description: B2 x-cell index corresponding to the first edge of the bypass between the inner and outer divertor (inner divertor, edge closest to target). If non-positive, counted backwards from the X-point location in the lower divertor, and from the inner upper target in the upper divertor.

switch.name: LPFRB_O
switch.description: B2 x-cell index corresponding to the first edge of the bypass between the inner and outer divertor (outer divertor, edge closest to target). If non-positive, counted backwards from the outer lower target in the lower divertor, and from the upper outer target in the upper divertor.

switch.name: LPFRT_I
switch.description: B2 x-cell index corresponding to the second edge of the bypass between the inner and outer divertor (inner side, edge furthest to target). If non-positive, counted backwards from the X-point location.

switch.name: LPFRT_O
switch.description: B2 x-cell index corresponding to the second edge of the bypass between the inner and outer divertor (outer divertor, edge furthest to target). If non-positive, counted backwards from the X-point location.

switch.name: J_HE_AT
switch.description: Species index of the helium atoms in Eirene. The code attempts to find a match by default.

switch.name: J_NE_AT
switch.description: Species index of the neon atoms in Eirene. The code attempts to find a match by default.

switch.name: J_H_AT
switch.description: Species index of the hydrogen atoms in Eirene. The code attempts to find a match by default.

switch.name: L_H_MOL
switch.description: Species index of the hydrogen molecules in Eirene. The code attempts to find a match by default.

switch.name: FUSION_POWER
switch.description: Fusion power occuring in core (including neutrons, in Megawatts).

switch.name: SPMP_HE_TO_D
switch.description: Ratio of He to DT pumping speeds (typically, 0.8).

switch.name: LPFRS_PMP
switch.description: Location of the pump. 0 no pump at all (default), 1 - lower PFR, 2 - upper PFR

switch.name: NPFRGRP
switch.description: Actual number of surface groups for PFR flows.

switch.name: LPFRGRP
switch.description: List of surface segments for PFR flows.

switch.name: IPFRGRP
switch.description: First positions in this list for each group.

switch.name: JPFRGRP
switch.description: Last positions in this list for each group.

switch.name: GPFRGRP
switch.description: Labels for surface groups for PFR flows.

switch.name: NNTRGRP
switch.description: Actual number of surface groups for neutral data.

switch.name: LNTRGRP
switch.description: List of surface segments for neutral data.

switch.name: INTRGRP
switch.description: First positions in this list for each group.

switch.name: JNTRGRP
switch.description: Last positions in this list for each group.

switch.name: GNTRGRP
switch.description: Labels for the neutral data surface groups.

switch.name: SPMP_NOM
switch.description: Nominal pumping speed.

switch.name: USER_FILENAME
switch.description: Filename where /USER/ namelist is stored.

switch.name: NX_SP
switch.description: Array dimension NX used in this namelist.

switch.name: NY_SP
switch.description: Array dimension NY used in this namelist.

switch.name: NS_SP
switch.description: Array dimension NS used in this namelist.

switch.name: SPUTTER_YIELD
switch.description: Contains the physical sputtering yield of species (is) at position (ix,iy) in element (:,:,:,1) and the chemical sputtering yield in element (:,:,:,2).

switch.name: SPUTTER_YIELD2
switch.description: Contains the energy physical sputtering yield of species (is) at position (ix,iy) in element (:,:,:,1) and the energy chemical sputtering yield in element(:,:,:,2).

switch.name: RESCALE_SA
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtlsa: ionisation rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_RA
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtlra: recombination rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_QA
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtlqa: electron cooling rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_CX
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtlcx: charge exchange rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_RD
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtlrd: line radiation rates of species (is).

switch.name: RESCALE_BR
switch.description: Scaling factors for rtlbr: bremsstrahlung radiation rates of species (is).
